questions.question: State and prove the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral.
questions.answer: A quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles. Sum of angles in each triangle is 180°, so sum of interior angles of quadrilateral is 180° + 180° = 360°.

questions.question: Show that the sum of exterior angles of any quadrilateral is 360°.
questions.answer: Each exterior angle forms a linear pair with an interior angle. Sum of all exterior angles = 360°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by the diagonals and sides to show diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by a diagonal to show opposite sides are equal.

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: Use alternate interior angles formed by a transversal through parallel sides to show equality.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence formed by diagonals to show they bisect each other.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show they intersect at 90°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a square are equal and perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence and Pythagoras theorem to show diagonals are equal and intersect at right angles.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its diagonals bisect each other.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence formed by diagonals to show opposite sides are equal, hence a parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem to show that opposite angles add to 180°.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rectangle if one angle is 90°.
questions.answer: Use parallel lines and transversal property to show all angles are 90°, forming a rectangle.

questions.question: Prove that a rhombus is a square if one angle is 90°.
questions.answer: Since all sides are equal and one angle is 90°, diagonals become equal making it a square.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a kite are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by the kite to show diagonals intersect at right angles.

questions.question: Prove that one diagonal of a kite bisects the other.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles on either side of the main diagonal to show it bisects the other.

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles between unequal sides of a kite are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show equality of these angles.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining midpoints of opposite sides of a quadrilateral forms a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Use coordinate or vector method to show that joining midpoints results in opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: Prove that a trapezium's midline is parallel to its bases.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry or vector method to show slope of midline equals slope of bases.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a rhombus is half the product of its diagonals.
questions.answer: Divide rhombus into four right-angled triangles using diagonals. Sum of areas = 1/2 × d₁ × d₂.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram circumscribed about a circle is a rhombus.
questions.answer: Use properties of tangent lengths from vertices to circle to show all sides are equal.

questions.question: Prove that in a rhomboid, diagonals bisect each other but are not perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show diagonals bisect each other and angle between them is not 90°.

questions.question: Prove that a cyclic quadrilateral has sum of opposite angles equal to 180°.
questions.answer: Apply inscribed angle theorem to prove sum of opposite angles is 180°.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a square bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Use congruence of right triangles formed by diagonals to show they bisect at 90°.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the squares of sides of a parallelogram equals the sum of squares of its diagonals.
questions.answer: Use vector method or coordinate geometry and Pythagoras theorem to show equality.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rectangle if its diagonals are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show that equal diagonals imply right angles, forming a rectangle.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if sum of opposite angles is 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem and converse property to show quadrilateral can be inscribed in a circle.
